agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly conflicting, non-negotiable requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, while Operations demands courier location never be shown to protect driver retention.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver assigned to transport a package from origin to customer destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's current GPS coordinates, updated continuously during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The time period during which a courier is actively en route to a customer with their order."},{"term":"Location visibility","definition":"The degree to which a courier's position is exposed to the end customer during delivery."},{"term":"Driver retention","definition":"The operational metric measuring the ability to keep courier drivers on the platform, directly affected by working conditions and perceived surveillance."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A delivery must have exactly one assigned courier whose location is tracked by the system for operational dispatch purposes.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"The system must know where couriers are to function as a logistics platform — this is internal operational data, independent of customer visibility.","testable_assertion":"Given an active delivery, the system always has a current location record for the assigned courier."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Customer-facing location data, if shown, must only be available during an active delivery for which that customer is the recipient.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Exposing courier location outside the scope of a customer's own delivery would be a privacy violation and data leak.","testable_assertion":"A customer can never see courier location for a delivery that is not assigned to them, nor after delivery is complete."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The level of location detail exposed to customers is a policy decision that must balance customer transparency with courier privacy and driver retention.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is the core policy conflict — the granularity of exposure is a business decision, not a system integrity constraint. A VP or cross-functional agreement can change this.","testable_assertion":"The system supports configurable visibility levels (exact GPS, zone-based, ETA-only, none) that can be changed without code deployment."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A delivery is in active transit","when":"The system records a courier GPS update","then":"The courier's location is persisted internally for operational use regardless of customer visibility settings."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer views their active delivery","when":"The tracking page loads","then":"The system renders courier proximity information at the visibility level configured by business policy (exact, zone, ETA-only, or hidden)."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A delivery is completed or cancelled","when":"A customer attempts to view courier location","then":"No courier location data is displayed and the tracking view shows final delivery status only."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A courier is not assigned to the requesting customer's delivery","when":"Any attempt is made to view that courier's location","then":"The request is denied and no location data is returned."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for an extended period (e.g., traffic, break)","expected_behavior":"If location is visible, customers may perceive the courier as idle. System should show contextual status (e.g., 'Courier is on the way') rather than raw coordinates to avoid misinterpretation and reduce driver complaints."},{"scenario":"Courier's GPS signal is lost or inaccurate","expected_behavior":"System must not show stale or wildly inaccurate locations. Fall back to last-known zone or ETA-based estimate with a staleness indicator."},{"scenario":"Courier completes delivery but app hasn't updated status","expected_behavior":"Location must stop being visible the moment delivery is marked complete. Race condition between delivery completion and location feed must favor privacy (stop showing immediately)."},{"scenario":"Customer screenshots or records courier location for misuse","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS creates abuse risk. Coarser visibility (zone or ETA) mitigates this. This is a factor in the policy decision."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Showing exact GPS may improve customer conversion and reduce 'where is my order' support calls (estimated 15-30% of delivery-related tickets). However, losing couriers due to surveillance concerns increases recruitment and training costs, which in logistics often exceed customer acquisition costs. Driver churn is typically the #1 cost driver for courier platforms.","operational":"Driver retention directly affects delivery capacity and service reliability. A platform that cannot retain drivers cannot fulfill orders regardless of customer satisfaction. Conversely, lack of tracking visibility increases support load and customer anxiety.","user":"Customers want delivery predictability — they need to know WHEN their order arrives, not necessarily WHERE the driver is at every second. Couriers want dignity and autonomy — constant GPS surveillance creates a hostile work environment.","risk":"HIGH. This is a stakeholder deadlock that will block delivery if not resolved at the executive level. Shipping either extreme (full GPS or zero visibility) will cause measurable harm to one side of the marketplace. There is also a legal/regulatory dimension: GDPR and labor privacy laws in many jurisdictions restrict employer surveillance of workers, which could make exact GPS sharing legally problematic.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Want delivery visibility to reduce anxiety and plan their availability. Exact GPS may exceed their actual need — most customers care about ETA accuracy, not coordinates."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected by surveillance perception. Exact GPS sharing to customers creates safety concerns (stalking, confrontation) and dignity concerns. Driver attrition is an existential risk for the platform."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Advocating for transparency as a differentiator. Their KPIs (CSAT, NPS) may improve with tracking, but the level of granularity needed is unclear."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Responsible for driver supply. They bear the cost of driver churn and cannot fulfill the customer promise without adequate driver retention."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but critically affected. Worker surveillance and location data sharing have regulatory implications under GDPR, CCPA, and labor law."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Two explicit, non-negotiable requirements from empowered stakeholder teams directly contradict each other. Neither can be satisfied without violating the other. This cannot be resolved at the engineering level — it requires executive arbitration and likely a compromise solution (e.g., zone-based tracking or ETA-only). Additionally, Legal/Compliance has not weighed in, which adds further uncertainty.","high_confidence_areas":["Internal courier tracking is needed regardless of customer visibility","Customer visibility must be scoped to their own active delivery only","The granularity of visibility is a configurable policy, not a hard technical constraint","Both stakeholder positions have legitimate business merit"],"low_confidence_areas":["Regulatory and labor law implications of sharing courier GPS with customers","What level of visibility actually drives customer satisfaction (exact GPS vs. ETA vs. zone)","Whether driver retention concerns are specifically about customer-facing GPS or about tracking in general","Executive-level priority: customer acquisition vs. driver retention"],"assumptions":["Both teams are accurately representing their stakeholder concerns","There is no existing executive decision or policy that resolves this conflict","The platform operates as a two-sided marketplace where both customer and driver satisfaction are critical"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates visible to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location to never be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements from two internal teams, both presented as non-negotiable.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers/Drivers","Customer Success","Operations","Legal/Compliance"],"options":["A: Show exact GPS (satisfies Customer Success, violates Operations — risks driver churn)","B: Show no location (satisfies Operations, violates Customer Success — loses differentiator)","C: Compromise — show approximate zone or map region (e.g., '3 blocks away') instead of exact coordinates, preserving transparency without surveillance","D: Compromise — show ETA countdown only with progress indicators, no map location at all","E: Make visibility configurable per market/region based on local regulation and driver feedback"],"recommendation":"Recommend Option C or E. The business need is delivery predictability, not surveillance. Zone-based tracking ('Your courier is nearby') satisfies the customer's actual need (knowing when to be available) while protecting courier privacy and dignity. This should be validated with customer research and legal review before implementation. Executive arbitration between Customer Success and Operations leadership is required before any engineering work begins."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot implement exact GPS visibility to customers as specified by Customer Success","business_rule_broken":"Directly contradicts Operations' non-negotiable requirement to never show courier location, and creates unquantified legal/labor risk"},{"violation":"Cannot implement zero visibility as specified by Operations","business_rule_broken":"Directly contradicts Customer Success's non-negotiable requirement for location transparency during delivery"}]}

Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly conflicting, non-negotiable requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, while Operations demands courier location never be shown to protect driver retention.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delive

content.thinking: Clear contradiction between two stakeholder teams. Both explicit, both non-negotiable, directly conflicting.

System should show contextual status (e.g., 'Courier is on the way') rather than raw coordinates to avoid misinterpretation and reduce driver complaints."},{"scenario":"Courier's GPS signal is lost or inaccurate","expected_behavior":"System must not show stale or wildly inaccurate locations. Fall back to last-known zone or ETA-based estimate with a staleness indicator."},{"scenario":"Courier completes delivery but app hasn't updated status","expected_behavior":"Location must stop being visible the moment delivery is marked complete. Race condition between delivery completion and location feed must favor privacy (stop showing immediately)."},{"scenario":"Customer screenshots or records courier location for misuse","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS creates abuse risk. Coarser visibility (zone or ETA) mitigates this. This is a factor in the policy decision."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Showing exact GPS may improve customer conversion and reduce 'where is my order' support calls (estimated 15-30% of delivery-related tickets). However, losing couriers due to surveillance concerns increases recruitment and training costs, which in logistics often exceed customer acquisition costs. Driver churn is typically the #1 cost driver for courier platforms.","operational":"Driver retention directly affects delivery capacity and service reliability. A platform that cannot retain drivers cannot fulfill orders regardless of customer satisfaction. Conversely, lack of tracking visibility increases support load and customer anxiety.","user":"Customers want delivery predictability — they need to know WHEN their order arrives, not necessarily WHERE the driver is at every second. Couriers want dignity and autonomy — constant GPS surveillance creates a hostile work environment.","risk":"HIGH. This is a stakeholder deadlock that will block delivery if not resolved at the executive level. Shipping either extreme (full GPS or zero visibility) will cause measurable harm to one side of the marketplace. There is also a legal/regulatory dimension: GDPR and labor privacy laws in many jurisdictions restrict employer surveillance of workers, which could make exact GPS sharing legally problematic.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Want delivery visibility to reduce anxiety and plan their availability. Exact GPS may exceed their actual need — most customers care about ETA accuracy, not coordinates."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected by surveillance perception. Exact GPS sharing to customers creates safety concerns (stalking, confrontation) and dignity concerns. Driver attrition is an existential risk for the platform."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Advocating for transparency as a differentiator. Their KPIs (CSAT, NPS) may improve with tracking, but the level of granularity needed is unclear."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Responsible for driver supply. They bear the cost of driver churn and cannot fulfill the customer promise without adequate driver retention."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but critically affected. Worker surveillance and location data sharing have regulatory implications under GDPR, CCPA, and labor law."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Two explicit, non-negotiable requirements from empowered stakeholder teams directly contradict each other. Neither can be satisfied without violating the other. This cannot be resolved at the engineering level — it requires executive arbitration and likely a compromise solution (e.g., zone-based tracking or ETA-only). Additionally, Legal/Compliance has not weighed in, which adds further uncertainty.","high_confidence_areas":["Internal courier tracking is needed regardless of customer visibility","Customer visibility must be scoped to their own active delivery only","The granularity of visibility is a configurable policy, not a hard technical constraint","Both stakeholder positions have legitimate business merit"],"low_confidence_areas":["Regulatory and labor law implications of sharing courier GPS with customers","What level of visibility actually drives customer satisfaction (exact GPS vs. ETA vs. zone)","Whether driver retention concerns are specifically about customer-facing GPS or about tracking in general","Executive-level priority: customer acquisition vs. driver retention"],"assumptions":["Both teams are accurately representing their stakeholder concerns","There is no existing executive decision or policy that resolves this conflict","The platform operates as a two-sided marketplace where both customer and driver satisfaction are critical"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates visible to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location to never be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements from two internal teams, both presented as non-negotiable.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers/Drivers","Customer Success","Operations","Legal/Compliance"],"options":["A: Show exact GPS (satisfies Customer Success, violates Operations — risks driver churn)","B: Show no location (satisfies Operations, violates Customer Success — loses differentiator)","C: Compromise — show approximate zone or map region (e.g., '3 blocks away') instead of exact coordinates, preserving transparency without surveillance","D: Compromise — show ETA countdown only with progress indicators, no map location at all","E: Make visibility configurable per market/region based on local regulation and driver feedback"],"recommendation":"Recommend Option C or E. The business need is delivery predictability, not surveillance. Zone-based tracking ('Your courier is nearby') satisfies the customer's actual need (knowing when to be available) while protecting courier privacy and dignity. This should be validated with customer research and legal review before implementation. Executive arbitration between Customer Success and Operations leadership is required before any engineering work begins."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot implement exact GPS visibility to customers as specified by Customer Success","business_rule_broken":"Directly contradicts Operations' non-negotiable requirement to never show courier location, and creates unquantified legal/labor risk"},{"violation":"Cannot implement zero visibility as specified by Operations","business_rule_broken":"Directly contradicts Customer Success's non-negotiable requirement for location transparency during delivery"}]}
